name: Natural Park of Urbasa-Andia – circular by Baizaundi
name_original: Parque Natural de Urbasa-Andia – circular por Baizaundi
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Monte Común De Las Améscoas traverse a diverse landscape characterized by dramatic karst topography and varied altimetry, ranging from 500 meters in the valley to over 1,100 meters in higher peaks. Situated between the Urbasa and Lóquiz mountain ranges, the region features extensive beech forests, open pastures, and unique geological formations. The Urbasa Natural Park offers a network of trails through its lush woodlands and plateaus, providing a scenic backdrop for rides. Best mountain bike trails around Monte Común De Las Améscoas The most popular mountain bike trail is Natural Park of Urbasa-Andia – circular by Baizaundi, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 45 minutes to complete, offering an easy ride through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Aldabidea Forest – Camping Perkasa loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba, a difficult 30.8 miles (49.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and explores the extensive Aldabidea Forest. Local mountain bikers also love the Raso de Ezkiza – Camping Perkasa loop from Améscoa Baja, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain including open pastures and woodlands, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Monte Común De Las Améscoas is defined by karst topography, ancient beech forests, and high-altitude plateaus. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Monte Común De Las Améscoas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Monte Común De Las Améscoas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: There are over 80 mountain bike trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas. These routes traverse a diverse landscape, offering options for various skill levels through dramatic karst topography and extensive beech forests.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The terrain in Monte Común De Las Améscoas is characterized by its dramatic karst topography, varied altimetry, and extensive beech forests, particularly within the Urbasa Natural Park. You'll find a mix of lush woodlands, open pastures, and unique geological formations, with trails ranging from 500 meters in the valley to over 1,100 meters in higher peaks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for different skill levels?
A: Yes, Monte Común De Las Améscoas offers mountain bike trails for all skill levels. There are 8 easy routes, 41 moderate routes, and 35 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners, intermediate riders, and advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: For families looking for an easier ride, there are 8 easy mountain bike trails available. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989981044/natural-park-of-urbasa-andia-circular-by-baizaundi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Urbasa-Andia – circular by Baizaundi&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 miles (21.7 km) long and offers a gentle ride through the natural park.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions I can see near the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural attractions. You can visit the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt; with its impressive waterfalls and pools, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobería Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are also a beautiful sight.

Q: Are there any viewpoints accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the area features several panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10522952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint – Ibiriku La Nevera de Dulanz loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.7-mile (23.6 km) route that takes you to the Mirador de Ubaba, offering stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive beech forests, and the variety of routes catering to different abilities.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 35 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldabidea Forest – Camping Perkasa loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.8-mile (49.5 km) path with significant elevation gain, exploring the extensive Aldabidea Forest.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that form a loop?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Monte Común De Las Améscoas are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5346035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raso de Ezkiza – Camping Perkasa loop from Améscoa Baja&lt;/a&gt; is a 30.0-mile (48.3 km) moderate trail that leads through varied terrain, including open pastures and woodlands.

Q: Can I find places to stay or amenities near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, there are amenities available. For accommodation, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3336483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Perkasa&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the area, often featured in route descriptions. The region also has charming villages where you can find local services.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The region experiences a bioclimatic frontier with Atlantic, sub-Mediterranean, and Mediterranean climates. While specific seasonal conditions can vary, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, so carrying sufficient water is advised.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: While the region is known for its natural beauty, specific regulations regarding dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, especially within the Urbasa Natural Park. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a safe experience for all trail users.
tips:
This area of Baizaundi is made up of primary iron deposits. For this they made crosses that are still preserved today.

The road is in good condition, although in winter there may be some landslides.

In the heart of the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, is this dolmen which, together with the La Cañada dolmen, constitutes two of the most important megalithic monuments in the interior of Navarre. It stands out for its cluster of very high stones that can reach 2.5 m in height.
The real name is Mirador de Ubaba although it is better known as Balcón de Pilatos. The name Mirador de Baquedano is not used, so it would be good to change the name.
